topic: Someone discovers their partner has been writing a novel about them
raw_output:
[story 1]

The laptop screen glowed with the harsh white light of a meticulously formatted manuscript. I found it tucked beneath a stack of old textbooks, a secret I hadn't known existed until that afternoon. Every carefully constructed sentence, every aching vulnerability laid bare on the page, was a direct mirror reflecting my own quiet misery. I stared at the words—words that described my habits, my fleeting moods, the precise shade of my disapproval when I was tired. A cold knot tightened in my stomach, a familiar, unpleasant clenching. When he finally looked up, his smile was bright, utterly unaware of the landscape he’d just mapped out. I just closed the laptop with a decisive snap, the sound echoing too loudly in the silent apartment.

[story 2]

I watched him typing, the rhythmic click of the keys a relentless soundtrack to my mounting frustration. It wasn't just any writing; it was an intimate excavation. He was detailing my insecurities with a surgeon's precision, turning my private anxieties into plot points. A strange, hollow sensation washed over me, like swallowing sand. I walked into the room, my footsteps heavy on the hardwood floor, and stopped just inside the doorway. "What is this?" My voice was flat, stripped bare of warmth. He jumped, startled, his hand hovering over the keyboard. The sudden tension in the air was thick enough to choke on. I didn't say anything, just let the silence stretch, letting the sheer weight of what I saw settle between us.

[story 3]

The discovery hit me like a physical blow. It was a collection of my unspoken moments, polished and presented for public consumption, all authored by the person I shared my life with. I retreated to the kitchen, gripping the edge of the counter until my knuckles turned white. The air felt thin, suffocating. I looked at the title page, the dedication—a line that spoke of devotion I felt entirely unreciprocated. My jaw locked so tightly I could feel the strain in my molars. When he finally entered, whistling a cheerful tune, I didn't bother to hide my reaction. I simply turned away, focusing intensely on washing a single dish, the movement slow, deliberate, and utterly devoid of any shared affection.
